X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaFirmwareCompliance_FirmwareComplian.description:
Firmware Policy configuration allows centralized control over firmware versions of all devices
including APs, switches, and gateways across various scopes. Firmware upgrade APIs will function consistently
in Central On-Prem deployments. In Central Cloud, API behavior may vary based on the customer’s deployment mode
and the device type. Full API support is available for the 5G Bridge device type while support for other
device types may differ.

allow-unsafe-upgrades.description: Enable non-failsafe module upgrade of CX switches. Ensure that the switch will not lose power, be rebooted again, or have any modules removed until all updates have finished

description.description: User maintained description for the firmware policy. System does not validate this for duplicates

enable.description:
Enable or Disable Firmware Policy configuration. If not enabled, firmware policy configuration
will not be inherited from parent scope configuration for this or any scope/device under this scope.

enforcement-schedule.description: Info related to scheduled firmware upgrade

reboot-schedule.description: Defines the reboot schedule of devices after downloading and installing firmware version

reboot-schedule-mode.description: Devices will be reboot immediately after downloading and installing firmware version

x-enumDescriptions.SCHEDULE_WINDOW: Enforce during maintenance window (NOTE: Currently Unsupported!!!)

reboot-schedule-mode.x-typeDescription: Reboot schedule modes: Immediate, After certain time, Recurring maintenance Window, Never

since.description: Device will be scheduled to reboot after this time once firmware download and installation completes
since.x-typeDescription:
The date-and-time type is a profile of the ISO 8601
standard for representation of dates and times using the
Gregorian calendar.  The profile is defined by the
date-time production in Section 5.6 of RFC 3339.

The date-and-time type is compatible with the dateTime XML
schema type with the following notable exceptions:

(a) The date-and-time type does not allow negative years.

(b) The date-and-time time-offset -00:00 indicates an unknown
    time zone (see RFC 3339) while -00:00 and +00:00 and Z
    all represent the same time zone in dateTime.

(c) The canonical format (see below) of data-and-time values
    differs from the canonical format used by the dateTime XML
    schema type, which requires all times to be in UTC using
    the time-offset 'Z'.

This type is not equivalent to the DateAndTime textual
convention of the SMIv2 since RFC 3339 uses a different
separator between full-date and full-time and provides
higher resolution of time-secfrac.

The canonical format for date-and-time values with a known time
zone uses a numeric time zone offset that is calculated using
the device's configured known offset to UTC time.  A change of
the device's offset to UTC time will cause date-and-time values
to change accordingly.  Such changes might happen periodically
in case a server follows automatically daylight saving time
(DST) time zone offset changes.  The canonical format for
date-and-time values with an unknown time zone (usually
referring to the notion of local time) uses the time-offset
-00:00.

upgrade-new-devices-immediately.description: When recurring maintenance window is selected, if a new device comes up, upgrade immediately

upgrade-schedule.description: Defines the upgrade schedule of firmware policy enforcement

since.description: firmware policy will be enforced after this time
since.x-typeDescription:

This type is not equivalent to the DateAndTime textual

x-enumDescriptions.SCHEDULE_WINDOW: Enforce during maintenance window (NOTE: Currently Unsupported!!!)

upgrade-schedule-mode.x-typeDescription: Download schedule modes: Immediate, After certain time, Recurring Upgrade Window

use-device-local-time.description:
Use date/time with reference to device's local timezone instead of common timezone as specified in firmware compliance.
When setting this to true, only use 2025-02-28T10:00:00.000Z format (without specifying offset) for upgrade-schedule or reboot-schedule

local-image-server-url.description: Local image download server URL to use instead of general image server

name.description: User maintained name for the firmware policy. System does not validate this for duplicates

x-enumDescriptions.LIVE: Live/Hitless Upgrade for AP and Gateway

delete.description:
Firmware Policy configuration allows centralized control over firmware versions of all devices
including APs, switches, and gateways across various scopes. Firmware upgrade APIs will function consistently
in Central On-Prem deployments. In Central Cloud, API behavior may vary based on the customer’s deployment mode
and the device type. Full API support is available for the 5G Bridge device type while support for other
